title: China's May shipments to the U.S. surge 35% to 5-year high as overall exports defy Iran war drag
description: Exports to the U.S. surged the most since March 2021, extending a rebound following a long streak of double-digit declines for the most of last year.

title: Trump booed before Knicks lose to Spurs at Madison Square Garden in NBA Finals Game 3
description: Trump's decision to attend Game 3 of the NBA Final was controversial for Knicks fans, some of whom feared he would jinx what has been an epic playoff run.

title: Perplexity plans IPO in 2028 regardless of what happens to Anthropic or OpenAI, CEO tells CNBC
description: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas' comments come after AI giant Anthropic confidentially filed to go public.

title: Pentagon expands list of China military-linked firms to include Alibaba, Baidu in fresh blow to diplomatic thaw
description: The Pentagon briefly posted a similar expanded list in February, then withdrew it without explanation before Trump's China trip.

title: Asia chip-linked shares recover after U.S. peers bounce back
description: In the U.S., chip stocks powered gains on Monday, helping the S&P 500 gain 0.3%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite advanced 0.86%.

title: Indonesia’s Bond Selloff Extends as Five-Year Note Yield Surges
description: Indonesia’s debt market selloff deepened as the nation’s five-year bond yield climbed to the highest level in more than six years.

title: Japan Still Can’t Escape the Old Normal
description: The BOJ has good reasons to hike, but is dragging its feet. A weak yen suits many people.

title: Apple's Siri AI Has Upper Edge vs Rivals, IDC Says
description: IDC Senior Research Director Nabila Popal says Apple's Siri AI holds two key advantages over other AI models: its access to personal contacts and strong focus on privacy and security. She also says Apple has caught up on AI strategy this year and its new features will enhance user experience. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Coking Coal Slides After Report on Push to Maintain Output
description: Chinese coking coal futures extended declines following a local media report that authorities in Shaanxi asked miners to maintain output, after a deadly accident in neighboring Shanxi last month raised supply and safety concerns.

title: Nifty Swings Rise as War Risk Unnerves India Stock Bulls
description: Geopolitics, costlier energy and uncertainty over global growth are weighing on sentiment.

title: Copper Holds Gain With Easing Iran Tensions, China Data in Focus
description: Copper held a gain from the previous session as tensions in the Middle East eased and investors awaited trade data from the world’s top metal consuming country, China.

title: World Cup Season Spurs Citi’s Bearish Call on Rates Volatility
description: Traders may have another reason to worry less about bond volatility spiking this summer: the FIFA World Cup.

title: GSK in Talks to Buy Nuvalent for More Than $9 Billion: FT
description: GSK Plc is in discussions to purchase cancer treatment maker Nuvalent Inc. for between $9 billion and $10 billion, the Financial Times reported, citing unidentified people familiar with the matter.

title: Maybank Handles $4.9 Billion Deals for Singapore, Malaysia Hub
description: Malayan Banking Bhd said the Johor-Singapore special economic zone will drive further expansion, with Malaysia’s biggest lender having facilitated about 20 billion ringgit ($4.9 billion) in financing and investments in the area in the past couple of years.

title: Citadel Securities Expands India Trading and Engineering Teams
description: Citadel Securities LLC, the market-making firm founded by billionaire Ken Griffin, is hiring more engineers and traders in India, as it seeks to expand in one of the world’s fastest-growing financial markets.

title: Bain Capital Makes Non-Binding Bid for Australia’s oOh!Media
description: Bain Capital has joined the pack of bidders for oOh!Media Group, the Australian outdoor advertising company said.

title: Xi Receives Lavish Welcome From Kim in North Korea
description: President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang to an elaborate welcome on his first visit to the reclusive nation in seven years In a meeting with Kim Jong Un, Xi vowed to deepen China's ties with North Korea. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Morgan Stanley Sees LNG Upside Risks as Asian Demand Picks Up
description: Liquefied natural gas prices are set to climb to levels not seen in more than three years as hotter weather in Asia and restocking needs in Europe boost demand, according to Morgan Stanley.

title: Trump booed in New York as he becomes first US president to attend NBA Finals
description: The catcalls came after ticketholders faced airport-style security to enter the venue at Madison Square Garden.

title: US adds BYD to list of firms with alleged Chinese military ties
description: The Pentagon list warns US firms of risks linked to working with flagged Chinese companies.

title: Why a U.K. By-Election in Makerfield Could Topple Prime Minister Keir Starmer
description: Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, is running for a seat in Parliament. It’s a vital step in a campaign to oust his party colleague Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

title: Crew Rescued After U.S. Helicopter Goes Down Near Strait of Hormuz
description: It was not immediately clear what led to the incident, which came at a time when the cease-fire in the war has grown tenuous.

title: I.C.C. Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended Over Sexual Harassment Claims
description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, faced an accusation of sexual harassment.

title: As Screwworm Cases Mount, U.S. Officials Ramp Up Response
description: The parasitic, flesh-eating fly has now been confirmed in cows, goats and dogs.

title: Trump warns Netanyahu: ‘You’ll be on your own’ if attacks on Iran continue
description: Trump's warning comes as Israel and Iran pause attacks and indirect talks to reach a peace deal continue.

title: US lists China’s BYD, Alibaba, Baidu as ‘Chinese military companies’
description: Chinese embassy in Washington, DC, condemns designation, calling it 'discriminatory'.

title: Botswana diamond slump hits miners living on the edge of survival
description: Botswana’s diamond-driven economy is under mounting strain as global demand weakens and production falls.

title: New York City crowd boos US President Donald Trump at NBA playoff game
description: US President Donald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on screens before Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

title: UN questions legality of Israeli forced evacuation orders in Lebanon
description: A UN spokesman says forced evacuation orders issued across southern and eastern Lebanon are nearly impossible to follow

title: ICC prosecutor suspended pending vote on sexual misconduct claims
description: Karim Khan rejects suspension as ICC governing body refers him to disciplinary proceedings before member states.

title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon despite halting attacks on Iran
description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu acknowledged a halt in fighting with Iran but will continue operations against Hezbollah.

title: Iran war live: Trump warns Netanyahu as Israel, Tehran halt fighting
description: Israeli strikes have killed 3,637 people in Lebanon since March, with 11,188 wounded, the Health Ministry says.

title: Alibaba and WuXi AppTec decline in Hong Kong after addition to US blacklist
description: Alibaba Group Holding and pharmaceutical contractor WuXi AppTec fell on Tuesday after the US placed them and several other Chinese companies on a blacklist over alleged military links. Alibaba slipped 0.3 per cent to HK$118.50 and WuXi AppTec tumbled 5.5 per cent to HK$114.60 in morning trading in Hong Kong. Other firms on the list, including electric vehicle (EV) maker Nio and tech giant Baidu, shrugged off the Trump administration’s move and rose after trading began. The Hang Seng Index eased...

title: Hong Kong must be ready for Chinese firms’ ‘great repatriation’ of wealth
description: In my travels around the world, I’m often asked if the best days of Hong Kong are behind us. My answer is always an emphatic “no” – and this is not merely a pep talk. When you step back and take a broad macroeconomic view, it becomes clear that, structurally speaking, our greatest opportunities lie just ahead. While many commentators scramble to draw parallels between Japan’s “lost decades” and China’s economic headwinds, they miss a far more apt and profound comparison: the phenomenon known as...

title: MiniMax once led Zhipu in Hong Kong’s AI stock race. How the tables have turned
description: When Chinese artificial intelligence developers Zhipu AI and MiniMax made their debuts on the Hong Kong stock exchange in January, investors initially saw one as a better bet than the other. Beijing-based Zhipu closed its first trading day on January 8 with a market capitalisation of HK$57.9 billion (US$7.4 billion). A day later, Shanghai-based MiniMax listed at HK$106.7 billion, almost twice as large. Five months on, however, Zhipu – traded as Knowledge Atlas Technology – has surged ahead. At...

title: How Fake News Could Send Oil Prices Soaring
description: Oil has been surging since the beginning of the Iran war, taking barrels off the market—but the market has no way of knowing, in real time, whether the next disruption it reacts to actually happened because energy prices don’t wait for confirmation. They move on the first credible signal, and in a conflict where strikes, explosions, and conflicting reports are constant, a fabricated event doesn’t need to prove it’s real; it just needs to fit the pattern long enough to be priced. That’s the gap Hydaway Digital (TSXV:HIDE,…

title: China’s Subsidy Machine Is Reshaping Global Capitalism
description: The COVID-19 pandemic and the geopolitical conflicts to follow exposed severe weaknesses in global supply networks, prompting governments, caught off guard and complacent, to pour money into critical sectors like semiconductors, critical minerals, and pharmaceuticals to prevent future shortages and reduce dependence on geopolitical rivals. Consequently, governments across the globe have increasingly been doling out state subsidies to local firms in a bid to secure supply chains, accelerate the transition to green energy, and protect domestic manufacturing…
